title: OffsetFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing Offset features in a component and supports the ability to create new Offset features.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates an OffsetFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the offset feature you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the OffsetFeatureInput object to create the feature.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified offset feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified offset feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of Offset features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Offset Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new offset feature

add.title: OffsetFeatures.add Method
add.return_description: Returns the newly created OffsetFeature object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A FeatureInput object that defines the desired offset feature. Use the createInput method to create a new OffsetFeatureInput object and then use methods on it (the OffsetFeatureInput object) to define the offset feature.

related_samples.name: offsetFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the offsetFeatures.add method. This is the equivalent of the Offset command in the SURFACE tab.

related_samples.name: Offset Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new offset feature

classType.title: OffsetFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: OffsetFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates an OffsetFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the offset feature you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the OffsetFeatureInput object to create the feature.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = offsetFeatures_var.createInput(entities, distance, operation)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = offsetFeatures_var.createInput(entities, distance, operation, isChainSelection)

createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created OffsetFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: entities
parameters.description: An ObjectCollection containing the BRepFace objects to offset. Additional faces may be automatically used depending on the value of the isChainSelection argument. Input faces need not be from the same body.

parameters.name: distance
parameters.description: ValueInput object that defines the offset distance. A positive value is in the positive normal direction of the face being offset.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: The feature operation to perform. 'NewBodyFeatureOperation' and 'NewComponentFeatureOperation' are the options supported.

parameters.name: isChainSelection
parameters.description: A boolean value for setting whether or not faces that are tangentially connected to the input faces (if any) will be included in the offset. The default value is true.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

related_samples.name: offsetFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the offsetFeatures.add method. This is the equivalent of the Offset command in the SURFACE tab.

related_samples.name: Offset Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new offset feature

item.title: OffsetFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified offset feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: OffsetFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified offset feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: OffsetFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of Offset features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = offsetFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: OffsetFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = offsetFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: OffsetFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = offsetFeatures_var.objectType
